target: Verdict taxonomy — sections.json part2 (Misleading intro, Self-Contradicted grouping note) + wins.json (WIN-043, WIN-060 findings)
executive_summary: The six verdict categories are conceptually sound and should be preserved as-is. No new category is needed. The three issues (ISS-442, ISS-443, ISS-445) are all addressed through targeted prose additions and two finding-text patches — no verdict reclassifications required. The curmudgeon's strongest insight is that lumping non-discriminating predictions (WIN-048, WIN-052, WIN-055) under 'Misleading' alongside genuine data fabrication (WIN-021) implies deception where only methodological irrelevance exists. This is fixed by adding a subcategory-clarifying paragraph to section 2.6, not by splitting the category.

design_decisions.no_new_verdict_category: Adding a 7th category ('Non-Discriminating') would require re-evaluating ~15 WINs, regenerating all tallies, and updating every document that references the taxonomy. The benefit (cleaner labels) does not justify the disruption, especially since our version history already shows frequent changes. More importantly, the EVALUATION GUIDE already makes the non-discrimination argument clearly — readers who reach it understand the concept. The fix is to bring that framing forward into the Misleading section header.
design_decisions.no_verdict_reclassifications: The curmudgeon suggested moving WIN-043/060 to Misleading (for duplication) or cleaning their finding text. We choose cleaning the finding text. Reason: the PRIMARY issue with both WINs is that standard physics explains the observation. The secondary duplication note ('covered in WIN-007', 'duplicate of WIN-004/005') is an editorial cross-reference, not the verdict rationale. Removing the duplication language from the finding and verdict text makes them internally consistent with 'Std Model Explains'. The curmudgeon's alternative (reclassify to Misleading) would be equally valid but creates more churn.
design_decisions.tidal_grouping_acknowledgment: The curmudgeon correctly identifies that five Self-Contradicted tidal WINs (045-051) share a single geometric argument. We already use detail_group to link them, and each detail card shows 'Related WINs' — but the summary-level prose doesn't acknowledge this. Adding one sentence to the Self-Contradicted section intro preempts the obvious dome defender objection ('you're doing the same count inflation you criticize us for'). This is the right move regardless of whether we agree with the objection, because acknowledging it demonstrates intellectual honesty.
design_decisions.other_side_of_the_argument: The curmudgeon raised a deep point: our taxonomy has no positive verdict category ('Dome Model Correct'). A dome defender would correctly note this reveals an assumption. We address this in the Evaluation Guide addition — not by adding such a category (no WIN would receive it) but by explicitly stating what would qualify. This turns a weakness into a strength: we've defined the bar, the dome hasn't cleared it.

patches.description: Add subcategory clarification paragraph to Misleading section (ISS-442)
patches.new_string:
2.6 Detailed: Misleading and Unfalsifiable

The "Misleading" verdict encompasses several distinct failure modes, and understanding the distinction matters. Some WINs in this category involve data misrepresentation — wrong source values, fabricated citations, or unit errors (e.g., WIN-021's rad/s² vs. rad/s confusion). Others involve count inflation — the same observation counted as multiple wins (e.g., WIN-041/042 testing the same SAA decay rate twice). A third group involves curve-fitting presented as prediction — adding free parameters to match known data, then claiming the match as confirmation (e.g., WIN-030, WIN-057). Finally, several WINs are non-discriminating — the dome and globe models both predict the same result, so the observation cannot distinguish between them (e.g., WIN-048, WIN-062). This last group is not accused of dishonesty; the observations may be real and correctly reported. The issue is methodological: a prediction that both models make equally well is not evidence for either. We use "Misleading" as the umbrella because in each case the WIN is presented as confirmatory evidence when it is not — but readers should note the range of failure modes within this category.

{{DETAILS_MISLEADING}}

patches.description: Add tidal grouping acknowledgment to Self-Contradicted section intro (ISS-445)
patches.old_string:
These WINs are cases where the dome model's own geometry — its firmament height equation, cavity dimensions, and distance formulas — produces predictions that contradict the author's claimed values. The model refutes itself before external data is even considered.

{{DETAILS_SELFCON}}
patches.new_string:
These WINs are cases where the dome model's own geometry — its firmament height equation, cavity dimensions, and distance formulas — produces predictions that contradict the author's claimed values. The model refutes itself before external data is even considered.

A note on counting: Five tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) share a single underlying geometric contradiction — the dome's local moon at 2,534 km altitude produces a single gravitational concentration point, not the observed two-bulge semidiurnal tidal pattern. Each tidal constituent (M2, S2, K1, O1, N2) is individually self-contradicted by this geometry, but the root argument is one: the dome's moon distance determines the tidal pattern, and the dome's distance gets it wrong. We count them individually because the dome model counts them as five separate WINs; we note the shared root because intellectual honesty requires it.

{{DETAILS_SELFCON}}

patches.description: Remove 'covered in WIN-007' cross-reference from verdict text — replace with non-discrimination framing (ISS-443)
patches.old_string: Ratio is mathematical consequence of published NOAA trajectory; covered in WIN-007.
patches.new_string: Non-discriminating: the 2.26× drift ratio is a mathematical consequence of published NOAA pole trajectory data. Standard core-mantle boundary dynamics produces the same anisotropy without dome geometry. The dome provides no derivation of this ratio from its own parameters.

patches.description: Remove 'Duplicate of WIN-004/005' from verdict text — the primary issue is non-discrimination (ISS-443)
patches.old_string: Non-discriminating: the dome extrapolates the same drift data as NOAA/ESA. It has no core-dynamics mechanism to predict drift rate or direction independently. Duplicate of WIN-004/005 data.
patches.new_string: Non-discriminating: the dome extrapolates the same drift data as NOAA/ESA. Standard core dynamics explains westward SAA drift without dome geometry; the dome has no mechanism to independently predict drift rate or direction.

evaluation_guide_addition.description: Optional addition to the Evaluation Guide addressing the curmudgeon's observation that our taxonomy has no positive verdict. Turns a potential weakness into a rhetorical strength.
evaluation_guide_addition.location: After the 'Are the same data being counted multiple times?' paragraph in generate-html.js (Evaluation Guide section, around line 742)
evaluation_guide_addition.proposed_html: Could any prediction receive a positive verdict? This review's six verdict categories do not include a 'Dome Model Correct' option. That is not an oversight — it reflects the empirical result. If a WIN had produced a measurement that the dome geometry uniquely predicts and standard physics cannot explain, we would have reported it. What would such a prediction look like? It would derive a specific, novel numerical value from the dome's own parameters (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric wave speed) before the observation is made, and the observed value would match the dome's prediction while contradicting the globe's. None of the {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs takes this form. The absence of a positive category is a conclusion, not an assumption.
evaluation_guide_addition.notes: Recommended addition, not a required fix for any open issue. Preemptively addresses the strongest methodological objection a dome defender could raise. Requires editing generate-html.js directly since the Evaluation Guide is not yet extracted to sections.json.

additional_recommendations.description: Reorder verdict legend from current arbitrary order to severity-grouped: (1) Self-Contradicted, Refuted by Data; (2) Std Model Explains, Misleading; (3) Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable. This makes the taxonomy's logical structure visible. Low priority — cosmetic improvement only.

Add a code_analysis cross-tabulation table to Part 4.6 showing verdict × structural tags (hardcoded, relabels_standard, post_hoc, derives_from_dome). Would make the relationship between code findings and verdicts transparent.

holes_addressed: ISS-442: Misleading subcategory clarification — added paragraph to section 2.6 distinguishing four failure modes (data misrepresentation, count inflation, curve-fitting, non-discriminating)

ISS-443: WIN-043/060 verdict boundary — cleaned verdict text to remove duplication language ('covered in WIN-007', 'Duplicate of WIN-004/005'), replaced with non-discrimination framing consistent with Std Model Explains verdict
ISS-445: Tidal WIN grouping — added acknowledgment paragraph to section 2.3 noting five tidal WINs share single geometric root argument
kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon is right that our taxonomy's application is inconsistent at the margins, and that a careful dome defender could construct a legitimate process objection. The six categories are well-chosen in theory but the Misleading bucket lumps methodological irrelevance (non-discriminating) with genuine deception (fabricated citations). Adding a clarifying paragraph is not a perfect fix — a proper solution would involve subcategory labels in the summary table — but it addresses the substance without the disruption of a full taxonomy overhaul. The curmudgeon's deepest observation is that our taxonomy has no positive verdict, which reveals our methodology assumes its conclusion. We address this head-on rather than hiding from it.
anticipated_objections: Dome defender: 'You have no positive verdict category — the taxonomy is rigged.' → Addressed by Evaluation Guide addition: we define what would qualify, no WIN meets the bar.
Dome defender: 'You count 5 tidal self-contradictions but it's really one argument.' → Addressed by grouping note: we acknowledge the shared root while noting the dome counts them as 5 separate WINs.
Dome defender: 'Calling WIN-048 Misleading implies we're lying about the CMB axis.' → Addressed by subcategory paragraph: we explicitly say non-discriminating WINs are 'not accused of dishonesty.'

Internal: 'Why not just create a Non-Discriminating category?' → Adding a 7th category would require re-evaluating ~15 WINs, updating all tallies, and risking the perception of instability. The clarifying paragraph achieves 80% of the benefit at 10% of the cost.

misleading_subtype_analysis.note: For reference — how the 21 current Misleading WINs break down by failure mode. This analysis informed the subcategory paragraph in P1.

data_misrepresentation.description: Source data wrong, units error, cherry-picked sub-component, or scope mismatch (claim says 'global' but data is regional)

count_inflation.description: Same observation counted multiple times, or credit claimed for contradictory outcomes (null + positive)

non_discriminating.description: Both dome and globe predict the same result; observation cannot distinguish between models. Some also relabel standard physics.

curve_fitting_internal.description: Adding free parameters to match known data, internal self-consistency claimed as confirmed prediction, or fit quality inferior to globe
